name: COLLEGIUM PHARMACEUTICAL, INC
business: Collegium Pharmaceutical, Inc. has developed, licensed, and acquired a portfolio of meaningfully differentiated products for use in the treatment of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and moderate to severe pain. The company commercializes Jornay PM, Belbuca, Xtampza ER, Nucynta ER, Nucynta IR, and Symproic in the United States. It primarily sells these products to wholesalers that distribute them to retail outlets, managed health care organizations, and government agencies.
sellsTo: retail outlets (drug store and supermarket chains and independent pharmacies)

exposures.sentence: Our mission is to build a leading, diversified biopharmaceutical company committed to improving the lives of people living with serious medical conditions.
Xtampza ER is an abuse-deterrent, extended-release, oral formulation of oxycodone, a Schedule II opioid.

catalysts.event: Generic equivalent of Nucynta IR approved under ANDA
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, a generic equivalent of Nucynta IR 50mg, 75mg and 100mg tablets was approved under an abbreviated New Drug Application ( ANDA ) filed by a third party with the FDA, which carves out pediatric use from its label.

catalysts.event: Hikma launches generic version of Nucynta IR
catalysts.sentence: As a result of the anticipated launch of the third-party generic equivalent of Nucynta IR, Hikma launched a generic version of Nucynta IR on February 25, 2026.

catalysts.event: Hikma expected to launch generic version of Nucynta ER
catalysts.sentence: Hikma is expected to launch a generic version of Nucynta ER in the first quarter of 2026.
In June 2024, the FDA granted pediatric exclusivity to the Nucynta Products for an additional six months, to January 3, 2027 for Nucynta IR and December 27, 2025 for Nucynta ER.
In June 2024, the FDA granted pediatric exclusivity to the Nucynta Products for an additional six months, to January 3, 2027 for Nucynta IR and December 27, 2025 for Nucynta ER.

tamClaims.claim: In 2022-2023, approximately 11.4% of children or 7.1 million aged 3-17 and 6% of adults or 15.5 million had a current diagnosis of ADHD in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2022 2023, approximately 11.4% of children or 7.1 million aged 3 17 and 6% of adults or 15.5 million had a current diagnosis of ADHD in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: The overall prevalence of chronic pain among adults in the United States is 24.3%. Additionally, 8.5% of the U.S. adult population suffer from high-impact chronic pain.
tamClaims.sentence: The overall prevalence of chronic pain among adults in the United States is 24.3%. Additionally, 8.5% of the U.S. adult population suffer from high-impact chronic pain that frequently limits life or work activities.

tamClaims.claim: In 2021, the economic costs of chronic pain in the United States were estimated to be $722.8 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2021, the economic costs of chronic pain in the United States were estimated to be $722.8 billion, including $530.6 billion in medical care costs and $192.2 billion in lost work productivity, which does not include the cost of care for institutionalized individuals (e.g., nursing home residents, prisoners), military personnel, or children, or the costs associated with caregiving.

tamClaims.claim: In 2025, there were approximately 133.2 million prescriptions for opioids written in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, there were approximately 133.2 million prescriptions for opioids written in the United States, representing a 2.4% decline from 2023 levels and including approximately 9.5 million prescriptions for long-acting/extended-release opioids, and approximately 123.7 million prescriptions for immediate-release opioids.

tamClaims.claim: In 2024, 7.6 million, or 2.6% of people aged 12 and older, reported opioid misuse in the prior year.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2024, 7.6 million, or 2.6% of people aged 12 and older, reported opioid misuse in the prior year as collected by the National Survey on Drug Use and Health sponsored by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration ( SAMHSA ).

tamClaims.claim: In 2023, the number of reported deaths involving prescription opioids declined to 13,026.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2023, the number of reported deaths involving prescription opioids declined to 13,026, an improvement from 2017 levels.
